actual_result: Lines 47-48 simplified to single processedSet; lines 147-148 collapsed to filename-only processedSet.has(file). Test suite passes. This PROP has now been marked implemented THREE times. First closure (031889f) was reverted by workspace-sync 4ac60b2. Re-closed in cbbba05. Reverted AGAIN by workspace-sync 42f776b at 2026-04-09T16:40Z, because the fix I wrote for workspace-sync.md (smart_copy helper) never propagated to the workspace mount — build.js publish's syncFiles list hardcoded only 4 agent prompts and excluded workspace-sync.md entirely. Root cause now fixed: build.js syncFiles auto-discovers every .md under monitor/prompts/**. The underlying digest-reviews.js fix was NEVER actually reverted (still live since f094909); only the PROP metadata kept getting clobbered.
verification_pattern: grep -q 'processedWinIds' build-scripts/digest-reviews.js && echo STILL_BROKEN || echo FIXED
summary: Remove processedWinIds legacy fallback that masks Cycle 2+ reviews
problem: Lines 147-148 in digest-reviews.js check processedWinIds (bare WIN IDs extracted from filenames) as a fallback for the isProcessed check. This means any Cycle 2 review (e.g., WIN-062.c2.json) whose base WIN ID (WIN-062) appears in processed-reviews.json gets silently skipped, even though the Cycle 2 review file itself was never processed. The code comment at lines 143-145 explicitly describes this exact bug.
escalation_history: Flagged in 9 consecutive tinker reports. Fixed on run 8 (commit f094909). Re-flagged on runs 8 and 9 due to tinker's previous_followup verification checking workspace md5 against main rather than grepping the target symbol. Closed three times: 031889f, cbbba05, and this round. First two closures reverted by workspace-sync; third revert was the proof that the smart_copy fix in workspace-sync.md never propagated because build.js publish wasn't syncing workspace-sync.md to the mount.

fix.rationale: Filename-only matching (processedSet.has(file)) is sufficient and inherently cycle-aware. WIN-001.json and WIN-001.c2.json are different filenames, so Cycle 2 reviews are never masked by Cycle 1 processing. The bare WIN ID fallback was a pre-cycle-awareness holdover.

testing: Run: node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js && node test.js — verify pending-digest.json still generates correctly and test suite passes.
risk: Low. The fix simplifies the logic and removes an acknowledged bug. No behavioral change for Cycle 1 reviews (already matched by filename). Only affects Cycle 2+ reviews that were previously incorrectly masked.
